questions.q: In Orson Welles' 'The Magnificent Ambersons' (1942), who was responsible for the controversial post-production re-editing that significantly altered Welles' original vision?
What was the primary reason Alfred Hitchcock selected 'Rope' (1948) to be filmed with continuous ten-minute takes rather than traditional scene-by-scene editing?
choices: To create the illusion of real-time action unfolding on stage
To reduce production costs by minimizing crew setup time
To showcase new camera technology that had just been invented
To comply with the Motion Picture Production Code restrictions on cutting

questions.q: In 'Singin' in the Rain' (1952), what was the primary reason that most of Gene Kelly's famous rain-soaked dance sequence was filmed using a water mixture rather than actual rain?
choices: To ensure consistent camera focus and prevent water spots on the lens
To maintain control over lighting and allow proper reflection
To protect the studio floor from water damage
Because the scene was shot indoors on a soundstage set

questions.q: What film noir classic did director Robert Aldrich adapt from Ernest Hemingway's novel 'Kiss Me, Deadly', and what was the significant change he made to the source material's ending?
choices: 'The Big Sleep' — he added a romantic subplot
'Kiss Me Deadly' — he added an atomic bomb revelation absent from the novel
'The Killers' — he changed the protagonist's occupation
'Out of the Past' — he altered the femme fatale's motivation
